title: 1st Conjugation
purpose: Train present-tense 1st-conjugation verbs as tiny, measurable atoms.
uiSignature: Six pronoun chips above the prompt card. Central verb card. Heatmap below. Coral highlight for removable infinitive ending / mutable slot during guided stages.
subdeckRules: Keep regular core verbs separate from irregular stem-change verbs.
For choose2/choose4/fullChoice, options must come from the same verb paradigm only.
Do not expose cross-verb distractors until at least typeFragment stage in mixed review, and even there only if explicitly enabled.

referenceTable.notes: Regular core deck should only include verbs where the user can mostly learn the person ending without a major stem change. Irregular verbs live in their own subdeck.

subdecks.title: Regular core 1st-conjugation verbs
subdecks.whyThisExists: This is the cleanest 1st-conjugation pattern deck. It excludes stem-change verbs so the user can get pure reps on person-ending retrieval.

verbs.translation: to walk / go for a walk

teachingRules: Preview must show infinitive, translation, pronoun strip, and full paradigm.
Choose2/Choose4/FullChoice options must stay inside the same verb only.
TypeFragment asks only for the changing ending when the stem is already on screen.
SentenceGuided must highlight the mutable verb region in coral/red.

atoms.translation: to walk / go for a walk
to walk / go for a walk
to walk / go for a walk
to walk / go for a walk
to walk / go for a walk
to walk / go for a walk

subdecks.title: Irregular / stem-change 1st-conjugation verbs
subdecks.whyThisExists: These verbs break the clean 'just swap the ending' intuition. They need isolated reps so the user can see exactly which irregular verbs are still slow.

verbs.teachingNote: Do not teach this as a pure ending swap. The present-tense stem changes: писать -> пиш-.
Keep this in its own subdeck. Show the ё forms explicitly: даёшь, даёт, даёте.

teachingRules: Never mix these verbs into fc_regular_core until the user explicitly enters mixed review.
Preview must explicitly show the stem change or ё-bearing form.
The analytics sidebar must surface these separately from regular verbs.

sentenceBank.ru: Я работаю в понедельник.
sentenceBank.en: I work on Monday.

sentenceBank.ru: Я никогда не работаю дома.
sentenceBank.en: I never work at home.

sentenceBank.ru: Я работаю в школе до вечера.
sentenceBank.en: I work at school until evening.

sentenceBank.ru: В понедельник я обычно работаю до шести вечера.
sentenceBank.en: On Monday I usually work until six in the evening.

sentenceBank.ru: Мы гуляем в парке в понедельник вечером.
sentenceBank.en: We walk in the park on Monday evening.

sentenceBank.ru: В пятницу они гуляют по центру города.
sentenceBank.en: On Friday they walk around the city center.

sentenceBank.ru: В четверг они редко гуляют в парке после уроков.
sentenceBank.en: On Thursday they rarely walk in the park after classes.

sentenceBank.ru: Мы часто гуляем после уроков.
sentenceBank.en: We often walk after classes.

sentenceBank.ru: Во вторник она иногда читает интересную книгу.
sentenceBank.en: On Tuesday she sometimes reads an interesting book.

sentenceBank.ru: Ты читаешь новости по утрам.
sentenceBank.en: You read the news in the mornings.

sentenceBank.ru: В пятницу они всегда читают статьи онлайн.
sentenceBank.en: On Friday they always read articles online.

sentenceBank.ru: Вы читаете газету во вторник.
sentenceBank.en: You read the newspaper on Tuesday.

sentenceBank.ru: В пятницу ты иногда слушаешь музыку дома.
sentenceBank.en: On Friday you sometimes listen to music at home.

sentenceBank.ru: В субботу мы слушаем подкаст на английском.
sentenceBank.en: On Saturday we listen to a podcast in English.

sentenceBank.ru: Вы слушаете музыку в наушниках.
sentenceBank.en: You listen to music in headphones.

sentenceBank.ru: Вы слушаете аудиокнигу в машине.
sentenceBank.en: You listen to an audiobook in the car.

sentenceBank.ru: В субботу он обычно играет в футбол с друзьями.
sentenceBank.en: On Saturday he usually plays football with friends.

sentenceBank.ru: Они играют в компьютерные игры.
sentenceBank.en: They play computer games.

sentenceBank.ru: В воскресенье он играет на гитаре.
sentenceBank.en: On Sunday he plays the guitar.

sentenceBank.ru: Они играют на барабанах.
sentenceBank.en: They play the drums.

sentenceBank.ru: Она пишет письмо в среду.
sentenceBank.en: She writes a letter on Wednesday.

sentenceBank.ru: В четверг я пишу сообщения студентам.
sentenceBank.en: On Thursday I write messages to students.

sentenceBank.ru: В среду мы всегда пишем тест по русскому языку.
sentenceBank.en: On Wednesday we always write a Russian language test.

sentenceBank.ru: Я пишу книгу.
sentenceBank.en: I am writing a book.

sentenceBank.ru: Вы даёте мне книгу.
sentenceBank.en: You give me a book.
sentenceBank.note: Constructed from the note paradigm only; this exact sentence is not in the notes but uses only learned vocabulary.
